dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
indicator: brooding over schoolwork during free time
text: The moment a free period ends, I start dreading the next one because it means more work to think about. There's no peace. It's a constant low-grade panic about the next task.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
indicator: brooding over schoolwork during free time
text: I have two modes: active schoolwork, and passive schoolwork brooding. There is no 'off' mode. Even in the shower, I'm solving algebra problems in my head.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
indicator: brooding over schoolwork during free time
text: My 'relaxation' is just switching between different school subjects in my head. History facts, math formulas, essay structures—it's a non-stop parade of curriculum.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
indicator: brooding over schoolwork during free time
text: I scheduled a 'do nothing' afternoon. I achieved nothing, but I also didn't relax because I was the entire time thinking about the nothing I was supposed to be doing for school.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
indicator: brooding over schoolwork during free time
text: My friends were talking about plans for the summer, and all I could think about was the makeup work I have to do. How am I supposed to plan a vacation when I can't even clear my head?

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
indicator: brooding over schoolwork during free time
text: I can't even have a normal conversation without my mind drifting to school. Someone says 'test' and I immediately start recalling definitions, even if my own test is weeks away.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
indicator: brooding over schoolwork during free time
text: My free time is spent mentally writing emails to teachers explaining why I need extensions, even when I haven't asked for one yet. I'm rehearsing failure.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
indicator: brooding over schoolwork during free time
text: I took a drive with no destination. Just me and the radio. But the radio was just background noise to the soundtrack of me worrying about my GPA. I turned around after 10 minutes.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
indicator: brooding over schoolwork during free time
text: I set aside an hour to play guitar. I played three chords and then started thinking about the graph I had to make for economics. The guitar is gathering dust.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
indicator: brooding over schoolwork during free time
text: I was at a party, and someone told a joke. I smiled, but my brain was back in the classroom, wondering if the teacher would curve the last test. I'm always in two places.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
indicator: brooding over schoolwork during free time
text: I used to love reading for fun. Now when I pick up a book, I start analyzing it like a text for English class. I can't separate 'for school' from 'for me.'

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
indicator: brooding over schoolwork during free time
text: Even when I'm doing something fun, a little voice in my head is critiquing my last assignment grade or planning my study schedule for next week. There's no true off switch.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
indicator: brooding over schoolwork during free time
text: I was at a birthday party, and I was the one staring at my phone, not for social media, but to check my school email. I missed the cake because I was waiting for a teacher's reply.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
indicator: brooding over schoolwork during free time
text: The worst part is the Sunday night simulation. All day Saturday and Sunday, my brain runs a simulation of Monday's schedule, and by Sunday evening, I'm already exhausted from the week that hasn't happened.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
indicator: brooding over schoolwork during free time
text: Game night with my siblings. I kept losing because I was spacing out, going over the formulas for the physics test in my head. They got frustrated, but I couldn't focus on the game.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
indicator: brooding over schoolwork during free time
text: I have a perfect attendance record for my own mental lectures. Even at the dinner table, I'm giving myself a presentation on the causes of the French Revolution.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
indicator: brooding over schoolwork during free time
text: I was on a phone call with my cousin, and I realized I hadn't heard the last two minutes because I was figuring out my class schedule for next semester. Again.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
text: I took detailed notes during lecture. I reviewed them. I made flashcards. I still failed the quiz. The information just doesn't stick. My memory is terrible, or my understanding is shallow. Probably both.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
text: The syllabus says we should be able to do X by now. I can't do X. Not even close. I look around the room and everyone's head is nodding during lecture. Mine is spinning. I'm falling behind, and I don't know how to catch up.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
text: The scholarship committee denied my application. The letter mentioned 'academic promise.' I guess my record doesn't show much promise. Just a steady, unimpressive performance. Exactly what I expected.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
text: The internship application required a writing sample. I submitted my best work. The rejection email said 'we found other candidates whose writing better met our needs.' Translation: my writing isn't good enough. Not for real-world use.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
text: I avoid answering in class now. What if I say something stupid? It's better to stay silent and be thought quiet than to speak and confirm everyone's suspicion that I don't belong in this lecture hall.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
text: I look at my transcript and see a pattern of B's and C's. No stars, no honors, no distinction. Just a solid record of average performance. That's my legacy: the definition of adequate. But I don't feel adequate. I feel like a failure.

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
text: We're reading a novel that's supposed to be a 'classic.' Everyone is making deep analyses. I'm skimming for plot points. I don't want to dig into themes or symbols. I just want the story. Even that feels like a chore, though.

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
text: I look at my art supplies gathering dust and feel a pang for who I used to be. The girl who would sketch for hours is gone. Now, my creative energy is zilch. Even electives feel like a drag. Nothing captures my attention like it used to.

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
text: I used to love the first day of school—new notebooks, new subjects. This year, I felt dread. Plain and simple. The shine is completely gone. I don't look forward to learning anything. It's all just a long, repetitive wait for summer.

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
text: I don't even look at the 'optional' challenge questions at the end of the chapter. What's the point? It's not like getting the right answer gives me a thrill anymore. I'm just collecting credits toward a diploma that feels meaningless.

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
text: My teacher said we could choose our own topic for the final project. Great freedom! My first thought was, 'Ugh, another thing to decide on.' The opportunity to explore something I might care about fills me with apathy, not excitement.

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
text: The teacher played a song from the era we're studying in history to set the mood. I didn't feel the mood. I felt the urge to check my watch. The connection between art and history that used to thrill me is completely severed.

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
text: I look at my calculus homework and feel nothing. Not frustration, not curiosity, just... empty. I copy examples without understanding because I don't see why I should understand. What difference does it make in the grand scheme of things?

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
text: I got a B+ on a paper I put zero effort into. My old self would have been furious. Now, I just shrug. It doesn't matter. The grade is just a number. The subject doesn't matter. Nothing about this place seems to matter to me anymore.

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
indicator: low motivation / thoughts of giving up
text: I've started calling school 'the daily grind.' It's accurate. It's just something I have to get through. The idea of 'learning for learning's sake' is a joke here. It's all about the GPA, the transcript, the next step.

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
indicator: low motivation / thoughts of giving up
text: College brochures look like fake promises. Everyone says 'it gets better,' but I don't see how more school is the solution. Maybe I'm just not cut out for this. Maybe it's time to admit I don't want this path.

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
indicator: low motivation / thoughts of giving up
text: My alarm goes off and my first thought is, 'Why bother?' Going through the motions feels so pointless. I'm just waiting for the bell to ring so I can go home and do nothing. Is this really all there is for the next four years?

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
indicator: low motivation / thoughts of giving up
text: I used to stay after school for help. Now I walk straight to my car. The building feels like a cage. The idea of spending more time here is unbearable. I'd rather be ignorant than forced.

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
indicator: low motivation / thoughts of giving up
text: Seeing my friends stressed over AP classes makes me tired. I just don't have that drive anymore. I'm taking the easiest classes next year. Why push myself for a piece of paper that might not mean anything in the end?

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
indicator: low motivation / thoughts of giving up
text: My parents' generation says 'school is a privilege.' It feels like a prison. A gilded cage, maybe, but a cage nonetheless. The privilege doesn't make the meaninglessness go away.

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
indicator: low motivation / thoughts of giving up
text: I used to underline key sentences. Now I just circle paragraphs randomly to make it look like I tried. The act of trying feels so performative. I'm playing the student role, but the person inside has checked out.

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
indicator: low motivation / thoughts of giving up
text: The phrase 'you'll use this someday' has lost all meaning. I feel like I'm storing information for a test I'll never take. My motivation tank is on empty. I'm just coasting on fumes until I can graduate.

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
indicator: low motivation / thoughts of giving up
text: I used to love reading. Now I can't get through a chapter without checking my phone. School has killed my curiosity. I just do the minimum to get by and then zone out. What happened to wanting to understand things?

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
indicator: low motivation / thoughts of giving up
text: I used to have subjects I loved. Now they all feel the same: chores. The joy has been leached out by deadlines and expectations. I don't know what I love anymore, if I ever did.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
indicator: lower expectations for one's schoolwork than before
text: I don't bother with extra reading anymore. Why would I? I've already lowered my expectations for my own knowledge. If the test covers only the slides, that's all I'll learn. My curiosity has been replaced by minimalism.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
indicator: lower expectations for one's schoolwork than before
text: My mom found my old report cards with straight A's. She cried. I told her I've changed—I don't expect those grades now. I'm okay with B's and C's. My academic expectations have been lowered, and I'm not fighting it.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
indicator: lower expectations for one's schoolwork than before
text: I don't compare my current self to my past self anymore because it's too painful. I used to be the one with all the answers. Now I'm the one who doesn't raise my hand. My academic expectations have fallen off a cliff.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
indicator: lower expectations for one's schoolwork than before
text: I don't compete for grades like I used to. Where I once wanted to be top of the class, I now look at the syllabus and think, 'If I get a D, that's fine.' My standards have fallen, and I'm not even mad about it anymore.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
indicator: lower expectations for one's schoolwork than before
text: I used to get upset over a 89%. Now I'm relieved with a 70%. That change in what I consider acceptable shows how much I've lowered my standards. I don't expect brilliance from myself anymore—just survival.

dimension_description: Diminished confidence in one's academic abilities and lowered self-expectations
indicator: lower expectations for one's schoolwork than before
text: In my journal last year, I wrote about becoming a valedictorian. Today, I wrote that I'd be okay with graduation. That shift—from lofty dreams to basic survival—shows how much I've reduced my academic expectations for myself.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: Trying to keep up with all my classes is like trying to drink from a fire hose. There's just no way to process it all. My head is so full of due dates and reading lists I can't hear my own thoughts.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: I get anxiety just opening my backpack. The number of textbooks and binders is a visual representation of how much I have to do. It's overwhelming.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: The workload isn't just hard; it's massive. I feel like I'm carrying a boulder up a hill. Every step is a struggle, and the hill keeps getting longer.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: I used to love learning. Now it just feels like a relentless series of obligations. The volume of work has killed any joy I had.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: I've stopped making plans. What's the point? I'll just have to cancel because I'll be buried in schoolwork. It's easier to just accept I'm not available.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: It's the small things that get me. Another reading to add to the list. Another problem set. They seem tiny alone, but together they're a tsunami.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: I just stared at my planner for twenty minutes. Between the history paper, chem lab, and two chapters of math, my brain just shut off. I don't even know where to start, and the pile of stuff just feels like it's going to swallow me whole.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: I'm falling behind in everything. It's not that I don't want to do it; I look at the total workload and just feel a massive wave of defeat before I even start.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: There's no 'later.' Later is just when more things are due. I feel like I'm perpetually catching a falling knife. It's exhausting.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: I'm surviving, not thriving. My entire existence revolves around school to-do lists. There's no part of my day that isn't touched by the workload.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: I'm not sleeping well because my mind won't stop going over all the things I need to do. It's a constant, low-grade panic about being buried in work.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: My motivation is dead because the task list is endless. Why start one thing when five others are screaming for attention? It's a recipe for paralysis.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: I'm tired all the time, but it's a mental tiredness. My body is fine; my mind is exhausted from the constant processing of academic demands.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: The pressure isn't about one big exam. It's the quiet, daily grind of assignment after assignment piling up. That's what wears me down.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: I sat down to study for one test and realized I have a project, a quiz, and a paper all in the same week. The stress is physical. My chest feels tight just looking at my syllabus.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: I'm starting to hand in late work because I can't physically do it all on time. It's not a choice; it's a math problem where the required hours exceed the available hours.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: I'm just plodding through. There's no enthusiasm, just a grim determination to survive the next deadline. The workload is a constant fog.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: I feel like I'm failing at life management because schoolwork takes up all the cognitive bandwidth. Planning a meal feels complicated when your mind is full of deadlines.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: I feel like I'm constantly running on a treadmill that's getting faster. New homework gets added before I finish the old pile. I'm tired before the day even begins.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: I feel like I'm on a conveyor belt. Work comes, I do it, more comes. There's no pause button. The relentless pace is exhausting.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: I've started to zone out in class just thinking about all the work waiting for me. It's hard to pay attention when your mind is drowning in a to-do list.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: The teachers don't seem to coordinate. It's like they think their class is the only one we have. The resulting pile is impossible to manage.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: It's 1 AM and I'm still working. I've been at this essay for four hours. Every time I finish a paragraph, two more tasks pop into my head. I'm just drowning in deadlines.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: The stress isn't about failing. It's about the constant, grinding pressure of the volume. It's a weight on my shoulders that never lifts.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: I feel like I'm failing at everything because I can't give any one subject the attention it needs. I'm spread so thin I'm transparent.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: My energy is completely tapped by 3 PM. Not from the day's activities, but from the mental load of knowing what's still left to do.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: I used to be organized. Now I just have a messy list of everything I'm not doing. The backlog is huge, and it feels impossible to catch up. It's suffocating.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: The expectations are so high. Every class feels like a full-time job on its own. Putting them all together is just unsustainable.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: I'm just so tired of feeling like I'm always behind. No matter how much I do, it's never enough. The pressure is relentless.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: I look at my friends in other majors and they seem to have free time. My field feels like a 24/7 responsibility. The workload is a beast I can't tame.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: My to-do list is longer than my arm. I've stopped writing it down because seeing it all in one place makes me feel panicky and defeated.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: It's the feeling of being stuck in quicksand made of papers and projects. I struggle, but I just sink deeper into the pile of work.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: My backpack weighs a ton, and so does my mind. Every class adds more. I'm not lazy; I'm just buried. I don't know how to dig myself out.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: My desk is covered in half-finished assignments from different classes. I jump between them, never completing anything fully because there's always something else. It's chaotic and exhausting.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: My study techniques don't work anymore because the volume is too high. Efficient effort is still not enough hours. I'm just overwhelmed by the scale of it.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: The constant switching between subjects is draining. My brain never gets into a flow state because it's always context-switching to meet the next demand.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: My brain feels like a browser with too many tabs open. I can't focus on anything. The sheer amount of work is paralyzing.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: My weekends are no longer for rest. They're for catching up on the mountain of work that built up during the week. It's a vicious cycle.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: I look at my weekly schedule and just sigh. It's packed from morning until night with academic obligations. There's no breathing room.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: I'm emotionally drained not from the difficulty, but from the sheer quantity. It's not a puzzle to solve; it's a quota to meet. And I'm drowning.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: Between AP classes and the job, my schoolwork is this monstrous thing that follows me everywhere. I can't relax because it's always there, waiting.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: The coursework has blurred together. It's not distinct subjects anymore; it's one big, amorphous blob of 'stuff to do.' I can't even separate it in my mind.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: I skipped lunch to try and get ahead. By the time I finished one assignment, three more had been posted. It's a never-ending cycle that I can't escape.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: I'm not procrastinating because I'm lazy. I'm freezing because the totality of the work feels so huge that I can't even begin. It's a form of overwhelm.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: I look at the week ahead and see nothing but assignments. There's no space for anything else. My personal life is on hold because schoolwork consumes everything.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: The list never gets shorter. I cross one thing off and two more appear. I'm just going through the motions, completely swamped by everything they expect us to do.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: Trying to manage my time feels useless. There are simply not enough hours in the day for what they assign. I'm perpetually behind.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: I had a breakdown last night because I couldn't figure out which assignment to tackle first. There's no hierarchy, it's just chaos. The volume is crushing.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: I've started to resent my textbooks. They're not tools; they're monuments to all the things I haven't mastered yet. The stack is a reminder of my overwhelm.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: My friends asked if I wanted to go into town, but I had to say no. I have three assignments due tomorrow that I haven't even started. The weight of it all is just too much to think about leaving my desk.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: I've started drinking coffee at 10 PM just to stay awake long enough to finish the chapter. It's a terrible habit, but if I don't, I'll fall behind. The irony is I'm so wired I can't sleep after, anyway.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: I don't remember the last time I slept through the night. Every time I close my eyes, I see the list of overdue assignments. I'm starting to think my bed is just another place to stress about school.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: My sleep schedule is completely ruined. I keep waking up at 2 AM worried about the lab report I haven't started. By the time I fall back asleep, it's almost time to get up for school. I feel like a zombie.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: I count the hours of sleep I get like a miser counts coins. Four here, three there. It's never enough to meet the minimum. The deficit is massive and growing, and I have no plan to repay it.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: My bed is my desk, my desk is my bed. The lines are blurred. I do my work under the covers with a flashlight because the house is quiet. There's no separation between rest and labor.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: The essay deadline is Friday and I have two more chapters to read. I was up until 3 AM trying to finish it, and now I can barely keep my eyes open in history. This cycle of no sleep then panic is just endless.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: Last night I stared at the ceiling for two hours, mentally outlining my English essay. I finally gave up and got up at 4 to write it. I feel like I'm running on empty and the tank has a giant hole in it.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: The most basic function—sleep—is completely hijacked. My own mind is the enemy, refusing to shut off. I'm at war with my thoughts, and the battlefield is my bed. I'm losing, and the collateral damage is my health.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: The stress is a physical weight on my chest, especially at night. I can't relax because I'm mentally running through flashcards. Sleep isn't restful; it's just a pause between study sessions.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: I'm not lazy. I'm exhausted. There's a difference. Laziness is a choice. This is a forced state of being. I want to sleep, but the work won't let me. So I soldier on, becoming less and less effective.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: I was up until 5 AM studying for my physics final. I know I should have started weeks ago, but between all the other classes, there just weren't enough hours. Now I'm too tired to even be nervous for the test.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: My teacher gave us a weekend assignment on Friday. That's a guarantee of a sleepless weekend. I'll be up Saturday night finishing it, then Sunday night stressing about the week ahead. No break. Ever.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: The pressure to get into a good college means I cram until dawn most nights. My body is exhausted, but my mind is stuck on calculus problems. I drag myself to first period, and it's a miracle I don't fall asleep.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: I've started taking melatonin every night just to force myself to close my eyes. But even then, my thoughts are on the Spanish verbs I need to memorize. My sleep is not my own; it belongs to the curriculum.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: There's no such thing as 'me time' anymore. That time is stolen by homework. The only time I have to myself is when I should be sleeping, and even then, schoolwork invades my dreams. I have no sanctuary.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: It's a vicious circle. I'm anxious about failing, so I study late. Then I'm too tired to absorb anything the next day, so I have to study even more. My sleep is the first thing to go, and it's never coming back.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: The weekend is not for recovery. It's for catching up on the work I couldn't do during the week because I was too exhausted from lack of sleep. It's a hamster wheel with no off switch.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: I'm in a constant state of low-grade fever from exhaustion. My immune system is shot. I get a cold and it lasts a month because I won't stop pushing. Sleep is for people who don't have 4.0 GPAs to maintain.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: There's a direct link: massive project assigned = zero sleep that week. It's a predictable pattern. I know I should pace myself, but with five AP classes, pacing is a fantasy. Exhaustion is my baseline.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: My teacher said I looked 'out of it' in class. I was. I was mentally still at my desk from the night before, trying to solve that one last problem. My body was present, but my consciousness was home, working.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: I'm so tired all the time. I lie in bed for hours, my mind just racing with all the equations I need to memorize for tomorrow's test. It's like my brain won't shut off, and I end up getting maybe four hours of sleep again.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: I'm in my senior year and it feels like I haven't slept properly since freshman year. Every major assignment triggers a week of insomnia. I'm just going through the motions, too tired to even care about the 'fun' parts of school.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: I think my brain has associated my pillow with stress. The moment my head hits it, I start remembering all the homework I didn't finish. I need to break the cycle, but the workload never eases up.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: The worst part is the guilt. If I go to bed at a normal time, I feel guilty for not studying. If I study late, I feel guilty for not sleeping. I'm trapped, and my sleep schedule is the casualty.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: They say teens need 9 hours. I'm lucky to get 5. And it's never deep, restful sleep. It's just a light, anxious doze until the sunrise reminds me of the test I have. I'm chronically behind on rest.

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
text: I'm learning about medieval plagues for a standardized test. The connections to modern pandemics are obvious but ignored by the curriculum. The meaning is there, but we're not allowed to see it.

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
text: I just finished another worksheet. Same format, different chapter. I look at the problems and my brain just shuts off. Why am I spending hours on this? What is the actual point? It feels like we're just jumping through hoops for the sake of jumping.

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
text: My engineering project uses idealized conditions. In reality, nothing works this perfectly. We're solving problems that don't exist. The gap between our work and reality is a chasm.

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
text: The 'real-world application' my teacher promised is just a hypothetical problem from a 1990s textbook. It's not real. I'm tired of the disconnect. What are we actually preparing for?

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
text: Sometimes I stare at my textbook and think, 'Who decided this was important?' Learning about chemical bonds seems completely irrelevant to my life. I'm just doing it to get a grade to get into a program I'm not even sure I want.

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
text: My economics class uses models that assume perfect rationality. That's not the real world. I'm starting to think the whole subject is built on sand. Why are we taught these simplified fairy tales?

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
text: We watched a documentary and answered comprehension questions. It was reduced to a quiz. The film's powerful message was lost. I question why we can't just watch and think.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: My aunt is in the hospital, and my family is taking turns visiting. I’m staying home to study for a final. They say I’m heartless. But if I fail this class, my scholarship is gone. It’s a terrible choice, but I feel forced to choose school.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: My dad tried to talk to me about his job loss, and I was mentally drafting an essay response. I gave vague answers. He said, ‘You’re not listening.’ He’s right. My exhaustion has made me a terrible listener, and he needed me to be present.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: We had a family dinner, and I was quiet the whole time, just thinking about my upcoming test. My dad asked if something was wrong, and I just said I was tired. But it’s more than tired—it’s like I’m not even there anymore.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: My grandparents are visiting from out of state, and I’m spending most of the time in my room reviewing for finals. My mom whispered that they think I don’t like them. How do I say that the fear of failing is louder than my desire to be with them?

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: My sister tried to talk to me about her day, and I gave one-word answers because I was mentally editing my essay. She got mad and said I never listen anymore. She’s right. My brain is always on school mode, and it’s pushing everyone away.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: My parents fight about my schedule. Mom says I work too hard and need a break. Dad says I’m not working hard enough. I’m caught in the middle, and their stress over my stress is making our home feel tense.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: I missed my parents’ anniversary dinner. They said it was fine, but I could hear the disappointment. They worked hard to afford it, and I chose to study instead. Now there’s this quiet space between us that I don’t know how to bridge.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: I haven’t called my grandma in two weeks. She leaves voicemails, and I tell myself I’ll call back after I finish this chapter. But there’s always another chapter. She probably thinks I don’t love her anymore.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: My best friend got into her dream college and wanted to celebrate. I was happy for her, but I also felt this huge wave of envy because I was too stressed to even enjoy it. She noticed I wasn’t enthusiastic. I’m becoming a bad friend.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: My little sister drew me a picture and left it on my desk to cheer me up. I saw it, but I was so focused on my notes that I didn’t even say thank you properly. She looked so sad. I’m failing her, just like I’m failing everyone else.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: My roommate complains that I’m always in the library. He says our apartment feels empty. I try to explain that if I don’t put in the hours, I’ll drown. But he doesn’t see that I’m drowning even when I’m here, studying.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: I haven’t seen my best friend in person in two months. We keep making plans, but something always comes up with school. She says I’m ‘too busy for her now.’ It’s true, and saying it out loud makes me feel like the worst friend ever.
